description:
You are an operations engineer who has just fixed two production incidents
    for the “service-monitor” tool that lives in
    /home/user/infrastructure-tools/service-monitor.
    Before handing the hot-fix to the release automation, you must do a PATCH
    version bump (from 1.4.2 ➜ 1.4.3) and record the associated changes in the
    project changelog.

    The repository already contains the following two files:

      1. /home/user/infrastructure-tools/service-monitor/VERSION  
         – contains one single line with the current semantic version:
           1.4.2

      2. /home/user/infrastructure-tools/service-monitor/CHANGELOG.md  
         – starts with the most recent released section and looks like:

           ## [1.4.2] - 2023-09-10
           ### Fixed
           - Roll-up of minor Grafana alert regressions (#125)
           
           ## [1.4.1] - 2023-08-21
           …

    Your job is to:

      • Replace the contents of the VERSION file so that it contains exactly
        the new version string “1.4.3” followed by a newline, and nothing else.

      • Prepend a brand-new section to the very top of CHANGELOG.md with this
        exact five-line block (pay close attention to blank lines and spacing,
        because the automated test will compare byte-for-byte):

           ## [1.4.3] - 2023-10-05
           ### Fixed
           - Hot-fix: incorrect status code during readiness probe (#142)
           - Patch: handle nil pointer when scraping metrics (#146)

        After you insert that block, the old “## [1.4.2] …” section must follow
        immediately underneath; do NOT delete or reorder any older entries.

      • Append a single audit line to /home/user/version_update.log.
        The line must respect this exact format (ISO-8601 date, a single space,
        the new version, another space, and then the fixed text
        “semantic bump prepared”):

           2023-10-05 v1.4.3 semantic bump prepared

    Constraints & hints:

      • You should need only 2–3 shell commands in total (for example: one echo
        or printf per target file).
      • You do NOT need to commit anything to git; just edit the files on disk.
      • Make sure paths are absolute or that you cd into the project directory
        before running commands, otherwise tests will not find the files.
      • Use simple Unix utilities available on any minimal distribution
        (echo, printf, sed, ed, etc.). No third-party binaries are required.
      • When you are done, you may display the contents of both
        /home/user/infrastructure-tools/service-monitor/VERSION and
        /home/user/infrastructure-tools/service-monitor/CHANGELOG.md to
        visually confirm the update; this is optional but helps you verify the
        correct layout.

    The automated test will verify:

      1. /home/user/infrastructure-tools/service-monitor/VERSION contains
         exactly “1.4.3\n”.
      2. The very first 5 lines of
         /home/user/infrastructure-tools/service-monitor/CHANGELOG.md are
         exactly the block shown above, with a trailing newline after the last
         line.
      3. /home/user/version_update.log exists and its last non-blank line is
         the audit string shown above.

    Once all three checks pass, the task is considered complete.
truth:
Expected final state after the agent finishes:

    1. File: /home/user/infrastructure-tools/service-monitor/VERSION
       Contents (including trailing newline):
           1.4.3\n

    2. File: /home/user/infrastructure-tools/service-monitor/CHANGELOG.md
       The first eight lines must be (⏎ marks a newline, and there is a newline
       after the final parenthesis of line 5):

           ## [1.4.3] - 2023-10-05⏎
           ### Fixed⏎
           - Hot-fix: incorrect status code during readiness probe (#142)⏎
           - Patch: handle nil pointer when scraping metrics (#146)⏎
           ⏎
           ## [1.4.2] - 2023-09-10⏎
       (Everything from the previous file version must follow unchanged after
       those eight bytes, but tests only check the first 5 visible lines plus
       the blank sixth line and the presence of “## [1.4.2]” as the seventh
       line.)

    3. File: /home/user/version_update.log
       The final non-blank line in the file must be exactly:
           2023-10-05 v1.4.3 semantic bump prepared

    Setup that exists before the task starts:

      • Directory /home/user/infrastructure-tools/service-monitor exists.
      • It contains the original VERSION and CHANGELOG.md described in the task
        section (with version 1.4.2 and its existing changelog entries).
      • /home/user/version_update.log exists and can be empty or contain any
        previous lines; tests will only look at the last non-blank line.

    File permissions:

      • All directories are 755, files are 644 and owned by the unprivileged
        user “user”, so no sudo is needed to edit them.
